Lab 6: Observing Spores & Young Gametophytes
Observe spores and gametophytes using dissecting  & compound microscopes
Make drawings of male and hermaphroditic gametophytes
Label: spore wall, rhizoids, cell wall, photosynthetic cells, chloroplasts
Use 10X objective

Microscope Storage
Move stage into lowest position
Remove slide from stage clips
Make sure low power is in center position
Turn off light
Unplug, wrap power cord around base
Store in cabinet
(arm of scope should be facing out)
Simple Model of Cell Cycle
Cell Cycle Interphase = G1, S, G2 M phase = mitosis or meiosis

In mitotic cell division, the amount of genetic information does not change
In terms of chromosome number:
How can this be possible?

Before division, chromosomes replicate in S phase of Interphase
Chromosome
“ Sister ” chromatids
Centromere

Photograph of replicated chromosomes during mitosis:
Chromatid 1
Chromatid 2
Centromere

Basics of mitosis (nucleus only shown here):
Parent cell with 4 unreplicated
chromosomes
Parent cell with 4 replicated chromosomes
2 daughter cells, each with 4 unreplicated chromosomes
Mitosis

Onion root tip
Chromosomes take up pararosaniline dye and turn purple
Root cap
Meristem = area where cells dividing

Nucleus
Chromatin
Cell wall
Nucleolus
In Interphase Cells, Nucleoli Are Visible

Stages of Cell Division
Interphase occurs prior to mitosis
Chromosomes will not be visible; you will only see chromatin, but not individual chromosomes
S phase is when chromosomes become replicated

Stages of Mitosis
Prophase & prometaphase are the first stages of mitosis
Chromosomes begin to condense and become visible
Nuclear membrane breaks apart (prometaphase)
Special spindle begins to form
Directs chromosome movements
Made of microtubules
No centrosomes in a plant cell

Stages of Mitosis
Metaphase : chromosomes align in middle of cell
Chromosomes are attached to fibers of spindle at kinetochores on centromere
Attachment from both sides causes chromosomes to align in center of cell.

Stages of Mitosis
Anaphase
Chromatids separate and migrate to opposite ends of cell
Pulled by spindle fibers

Stages of Mitosis
Telophase
Chromosomes stop moving
New nuclei formed
Chromosomes unwind/ decondense
Cell usually goes on to divide the cytoplasm - cytokinesis
Stages of Mitosis in a Plant Cell

Cytokinesis: Division of the Cytoplasm
Cell plate
Phragmoplast
[Speaker Notes] Phragmoplast: cytoskeleton structure (microtubules, microfilaments, and ER elements) that assist in cell plate formation in most plants

Cytokinesis in Plant Cells
Important terms:
Phragmoplast (made of microtubules)
Vesicles
Cell plate
Microtubules of
the phragmoplast
Cell plate
5  m
Microtubules direct vesicles to center of phragmoplast ,
where they fuse to divide the cell in two

Coordinate with another person at your lab bench.  Both of you will record data.  Make a table in your notebook that has two columns, Cell cycle stage , and Number of cells .  The possible cell cycle stages are interphase, prophase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase.
Using the 10X objective, record the cell stage for every cell visible in your field of view. Once you have recorded the counts for that field of view, have your partner look at the same cells to verify the accuracy of your data. Move to a new field of view and repeat these steps. Sum the data from all three fields of view.
Convert your counts to proportions and record your proportions on the front board with the data from all other student teams. Example calculation:  You observe a total of 205 cells.  If 31 of those were in anaphase, then the proportion of cells in anaphase is 31/205 = 0.151.
Collect data on number of cells in each cell cycle stage (pg. 6-3)
You will use these data to answer questions on pg. 6-4

Calculate proportion of cells in each stage

Onion chromosomes are larger than whitefish chromosomes
Animal (whitefish) cells have asters white plant (onion) cells don’t
asters

Comparison between cytokinesis in plant and animal cells
Cell plate
Phragmoplast
Plant cells use cell plate to divide cytoplasm while animal cells use cleavage furrow to do so
Onion
White fish
Cell wall
Cell membrane
Plant cells have cell membrane too, but their regular cell shape is due to the rigid cell wall.
Animal cells don’t have a cell wall therefore the shape is often more round.
